{
  "extract_plan": {
    "section_map": [
      {"heading": "Introduction (big bets on AI based on instinct)", "location": "lede"},
      {"heading": "Why is organizational AI readiness important?", "location": "why-is-organizational-ai-readiness-important"},
      {"heading": "The 6 core pillars of organizational AI readiness", "location": "the-6-core-pillars-of-organizational-ai-readiness"},
      {"heading": "1. Data, governance and privacy", "location": "1-data-governance-and-privacy"},
      {"heading": "2. Leadership vision and strategy", "location": "2-leadership-vision-and-strategy"},

      {"heading": "Step 1: Segment the organization by roles or levels", "location": "step-1-segment-the-organization-by-roles-or-levels"},
      {"heading": "Step 2: Assess readiness across key dimensions", "location": "step-2-assess-readiness-across-key-dimensions"},
      {"heading": "Step 3: Identify your top readiness constraints", "location": "step-3-identify-your-top-readiness-constraints"},
      {"heading": "Step 4: Pick targeted interventions", "location": "step-4-pick-targeted-interventions"},
      {"heading": "The role of change management in organizational AI readiness", "location": "the-role-of-change-management-in-organizational-ai-readiness"},
      {"heading": "Organizational AI readiness challenges", "location": "organizational-ai-readiness-challenges"},
      {"heading": "How to improve organizational AI readiness (30-60-90)", "location": "how-to-improve-organizational-ai-readiness"},

    ],
    "concept_inventory": [
      "organizational AI readiness",

  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c_org_ai_readiness", "name": "Organizational AI readiness", "aliases": ["AI readiness"], "description": "An organization's preparedness across people, processes, and governance to adopt, scale, and govern AI, distinct from having AI tools or infrastructure.", "grounding_span": "Organizational AI readiness encourages executives and leaders to look beyond tools and infrastructure alone before proceeding with AI implementations.", "location": "lede"},
    {"id": "c_ai_adoption", "name": "AI adoption", "aliases": ["adoption"], "description": "Whether and how effectively, consistently, and ethically people actually use AI tools to deliver outcomes.", "grounding_span": "the success of AI initiatives depends far more on whether the organization’s people are prepared to adopt, trust, and use it than on the AI technologies themselves", "location": "why-is-organizational-ai-readiness-important"},
    {"id": "c_data_governance", "name": "Data, governance and privacy", "aliases": ["data governance"], "description": "Practices ensuring data accuracy, accessibility, security, and regulatory compliance as a foundation for AI readiness.", "grounding_span": "AI is only as effective as the data it relies on, making strong data governance and privacy practices foundational to readiness.", "location": "1-data-governance-and-privacy"},
    {"id": "c_data_quality", "name": "Data quality", "aliases": [], "description": "The accuracy and reliability of underlying data, whose absence causes AI outputs to become unreliable.", "grounding_span": "Without this foundation, AI outputs become unreliable, and trust in AI technologies erodes.", "location": "1-data-governance-and-privacy"},
    {"id": "c_leadership_vision", "name": "Leadership vision and strategy", "aliases": [], "description": "A clear vision from leadership defining priority use cases and aligning AI initiatives to business outcomes.", "grounding_span": "A clear, compelling vision from leadership sets the direction for how AI will create value across the organization.", "location": "2-leadership-vision-and-strategy"},
    {"id": "c_talent_skill", "name": "Talent and skill development", "aliases": ["skills uplift"], "description": "A broad skills uplift including data literacy and specialized capabilities in data science, engineering, and AI governance.", "grounding_span": "AI readiness requires a broader skills uplift across the organization.", "location": "3-talent-and-skill-development"},
    {"id": "c_tech_capabilities", "name": "Technology capabilities and implementation approach", "aliases": [], "description": "A robust, scalable technology ecosystem—infrastructure, data platforms, integration, and AI tools—supporting AI development and use.", "grounding_span": "A robust and scalable technology ecosystem is essential to support artificial intelligence development and deployment.", "location": "4-technology-capabilities-and-implementation-approach"},
    {"id": "c_culture", "name": "Cultural behaviors and mindset", "aliases": ["organizational culture"], "description": "Cultural attitudes toward AI; encouraging experimentation and data-driven decisions accelerates adoption while fear or distrust stalls it.", "grounding_span": "Organizational culture plays a decisive role in whether AI is embraced or resisted.", "location": "5-cultural-behaviors-and-mindset"},
    {"id": "c_ownership", "name": "Distributed, cross-functional ownership", "aliases": ["shared ownership"], "description": "Shared ownership of AI across business, technology, risk, and operations functions.", "grounding_span": "AI readiness requires shared ownership across business, technology, risk, and operations functions.", "location": "6-distributed-crossfunctional-ownership"},
    {"id": "c_exec_behavior", "name": "Executive behavior", "aliases": ["leadership behavior"], "description": "Leadership conduct that is described as one of the strongest predictors of whether AI initiatives stall or scale.", "grounding_span": "including executive behavior — one of the strongest predictors of whether AI initiatives stall or scale", "location": "why-is-organizational-ai-readiness-important"},
    {"id": "c_trust", "name": "Trust in AI", "aliases": ["trust"], "description": "Confidence in AI tools, which varies by role, with executives reporting higher trust than frontline workers.", "grounding_span": "executives report higher trust (+1.09) and ease of use (+1.19) than frontline workers", "location": "why-is-organizational-ai-readiness-important"},
    {"id": "c_ease_of_use", "name": "Ease of use", "aliases": [], "description": "Perceived usability of AI tools, which varies by role.", "grounding_span": "AI tools are often perceived as easy to use and valuable, yet adoption still varies by role", "location": "why-is-organizational-ai-readiness-important"},
    {"id": "c_user_proficiency", "name": "User proficiency", "aliases": ["proficiency"], "description": "Users' skill in effectively using AI; cited as the most common AI adoption challenge.", "grounding_span": "the most common challenge is user proficiency (38%)", "location": "the-role-of-change-management-in-organizational-ai-readiness"},
    {"id": "c_human_factors", "name": "Human factors", "aliases": [], "description": "The people-side elements—communication, training, leadership behavior, culture—that account for the majority of AI implementation difficulties.", "grounding_span": "human factors account for 56–64% of AI implementation difficulties", "location": "the-role-of-change-management-in-organizational-ai-readiness"},
    {"id": "c_change_management", "name": "Change management", "aliases": [], "description": "The discipline of transforming human behavior so that AI strategy and governance translate into employee adoption, proficiency, and sustained use.", "grounding_span": "Change management makes AI readiness real by turning AI strategy and governance into employee adoption, proficiency and sustained use, where the value is actually realized.", "location": "the-role-of-change-management-in-organizational-ai-readiness"},
    {"id": "c_governance_control", "name": "Governance and control", "aliases": ["guardrails", "decision rights"], "description": "How clearly rules, guardrails, and decision rights are understood and experienced at different levels of the organization.", "grounding_span": "**Governance and control** – How clearly rules, guardrails, and decision rights are understood and experienced at different levels", "location": "step-2-assess-readiness-across-key-dimensions"},
    {"id": "c_impl_approach", "name": "AI implementation approach", "aliases": ["AI project approach"], "description": "Whether AI is perceived as strategic and coordinated versus reactive and ad hoc, and whether a level is involved in shaping use cases.", "grounding_span": "**AI implementation approach** – Whether people see AI as strategic and coordinated vs. reactive and ad hoc, and whether their level is involved in shaping use cases or just receiving tools", "location": "step-2-assess-readiness-across-key-dimensions"},
    {"id": "c_org_dynamics", "name": "Organizational dynamics", "aliases": [], "description": "Perception differences between levels in the organization.", "grounding_span": "**Organizational dynamics** – Perception differences between levels in the organization", "location": "step-2-assess-readiness-across-key-dimensions"},
    {"id": "c_training_capability", "name": "Training and capability-building", "aliases": [], "description": "Role-based education, coaching, and hands-on learning opportunities for building confidence to use AI effectively.", "grounding_span": "**Training and capability-building** – Gaps in role-based education, coaching, and hands-on learning opportunities for building confidence to use AI effectively", "location": "step-2-assess-readiness-across-key-dimensions"},
    {"id": "c_role_based_training", "name": "Role-based training", "aliases": [], "description": "Training tailored by role, proficiency level, and use case, contrasted with one-size-fits-all tool training.", "grounding_span": "Expand AI literacy across the organization with role-based training that helps people understand both the opportunities and limitations of AI, not just how to use specific tools.", "location": "how-to-improve-organizational-ai-readiness"},
    {"id": "c_readiness_scorecard", "name": "Readiness scorecard", "aliases": [], "description": "The output of scoring segments across dimensions, covering both organizational and human factors, not just technology.", "grounding_span": "This gives you a readiness scorecard that covers both organizational and human factors, not just technology.", "location": "step-2-assess-readiness-across-key-dimensions"},
    {"id": "c_readiness_constraints", "name": "Readiness constraints", "aliases": ["bottlenecks"], "description": "The most significant bottlenecks that limit adoption and value realization, such as low proficiency, lack of transparency, or unbalanced governance.", "grounding_span": "determine the two most significant bottlenecks that will limit adoption and value realization", "location": "step-3-identify-your-top-readiness-constraints"},
    {"id": "c_segmentation", "name": "Role/level segmentation", "aliases": ["segmentation"], "description": "Breaking the organization into key groups by role or level so readiness differences by level become visible.", "grounding_span": "Break the organization into key groups, such as executives, people managers, team leaders, and frontline employees, so you can see where readiness differs by level.", "location": "step-1-segment-the-organization-by-roles-or-levels"},
    {"id": "c_perception_gaps", "name": "Perception gaps between executives and frontline", "aliases": ["perception gaps"], "description": "Differences in trust and ease of use between executives and frontline employees that leaders may overlook.", "grounding_span": "executives typically report higher trust and ease of use with AI than frontline employees", "location": "organizational-ai-readiness-challenges"},
    {"id": "c_experimentation", "name": "Culture of experimentation", "aliases": ["experimentation"], "description": "An environment with clear guardrails that leaves room to test and learn, enabling responsible AI innovation.", "grounding_span": "Clear guardrails with room to test and learn are essential to building confidence and enabling responsible AI innovation.", "location": "organizational-ai-readiness-challenges"},
    {"id": "c_responsible_ai", "name": "Responsible AI", "aliases": ["ethics", "responsible use"], "description": "Integrating responsible AI principles into training, decision criteria, and project reviews.", "grounding_span": "Make ethics a visible priority by integrating responsible AI principles into training, decision criteria, and project reviews.", "location": "how-to-improve-organizational-ai-readiness"},
    {"id": "c_ai_roadmap", "name": "AI roadmap", "aliases": [], "description": "A simple, living roadmap outlining priority use cases, business outcomes, and key milestones.", "grounding_span": "building a simple, living AI roadmap that outlines priority use cases, business outcomes, and key milestones", "location": "how-to-improve-organizational-ai-readiness"},
    {"id": "c_targeted_interventions", "name": "Targeted interventions", "aliases": [], "description": "Actions chosen to directly address top readiness constraints for the right segments, with owners and timing.", "grounding_span": "Choose actions that directly address those constraints for the right segments.", "location": "step-4-pick-targeted-interventions"}
  ],
  "definitions": [
    {"id": "d_readiness_assessment", "term": "organizational AI readiness assessment", "verbatim_text": "An organizational AI readiness assessment is a structured way to evaluate your organization's preparedness to adopt, scale, and govern AI responsibly.", "normative_status": "industry", "as_of_date": "2026-05-22", "grounding_span": "An organizational AI readiness assessment is a structured way to evaluate your organization's preparedness to adopt, scale, and govern AI responsibly.", "location": "faqs"},
    {"id": "d_measure_readiness", "term": "AI readiness (measurement)", "verbatim_text": "AI readiness is measured by assessing both the technical and human conditions required for successful adoption.", "normative_status": "industry", "as_of_date": "2026-05-22", "grounding_span": "AI readiness is measured by assessing both the technical and human conditions required for successful adoption.", "location": "faqs"}
  ],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl_people_over_tech", "claim_text": "The success of AI initiatives depends far more on whether the organization's people are prepared to adopt, trust, and use AI than on the AI technologies themselves.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "the success of AI initiatives depends far more on whether the organization’s people are prepared to adopt, trust, and use it than on the AI technologies themselves", "location": "why-is-organizational-ai-readiness-important"},
    {"id": "cl_role_variance", "claim_text": "Executives report higher trust (+1.09) and ease of use (+1.19) with AI than frontline workers.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "executives report higher trust (+1.09) and ease of use (+1.19) than frontline workers", "location": "why-is-organizational-ai-readiness-important"},
    {"id": "cl_exec_predictor", "claim_text": "Executive behavior is one of the strongest predictors of whether AI initiatives stall or scale.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "executive behavior — one of the strongest predictors of whether AI initiatives stall or scale, according to our research", "location": "why-is-organizational-ai-readiness-important"},
    {"id": "cl_human_factors_pct", "claim_text": "Human factors account for 56–64% of AI implementation difficulties.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "human factors account for 56–64% of AI implementation difficulties", "location": "the-role-of-change-management-in-organizational-ai-readiness"},
    {"id": "cl_proficiency_common", "claim_text": "The most common AI adoption challenge is user proficiency, at 38%.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "the most common challenge is user proficiency (38%)", "location": "the-role-of-change-management-in-organizational-ai-readiness"},
    {"id": "cl_data_effectiveness", "claim_text": "AI is only as effective as the data it relies on.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "AI is only as effective as the data it relies on", "location": "1-data-governance-and-privacy"},
    {"id": "cl_culture_decisive", "claim_text": "Organizational culture plays a decisive role in whether AI is embraced or resisted.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Organizational culture plays a decisive role in whether AI is embraced or resisted.", "location": "5-cultural-behaviors-and-mindset"},
    {"id": "cl_checklist_barrier", "claim_text": "Treating AI readiness as a technology checklist only is the most critical barrier and becomes the root cause of many other challenges.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "This is the most critical barrier because it becomes the root cause of many other challenges.", "location": "organizational-ai-readiness-challenges"},
    {"id": "cl_perceptions_vary", "claim_text": "Perceptions and experiences of AI vary by role, making segmentation essential to get an accurate readiness picture.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "perceptions and experiences vary by role, so segmentation is essential to get an accurate picture and identify targeted solutions for each group", "location": "step-1-segment-the-organization-by-roles-or-levels"},
    {"id": "cl_investing_fail_value", "claim_text": "Many enterprises invest heavily in AI tools but fail to realize value due to unclear strategy, poor data quality, skill gaps, or cultural resistance.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Many enterprises are investing heavily in AI tools but fail to realize value due to unclear strategy, poor data quality, skill gaps, or cultural resistance.", "location": "why-is-organizational-ai-readiness-important"}
  ],
  "instruments": [
    {"id": "i_readiness_assessment", "name": "Organizational AI readiness assessment", "owner": "Prosci", "year": 2026, "method": "Segments the organization by role or level, then scores each segment across dimensions such as governance and control, AI project approach, organizational dynamics, and training and capability-building, producing a readiness scorecard.", "grounding_span": "It typically segments the organization by role or level, then scores each segment across dimensions such as governance and control, AI project approach, organizational dynamics, and training and capability-building.", "location": "faqs"}

    {"id": "m_org_dynamics", "text": "Perception differences between levels in the organization", "response_type": "score", "grounding_span": "**Organizational dynamics** – Perception differences between levels in the organization", "location": "step-2-assess-readiness-across-key-dimensions"},
    {"id": "m_training_capability", "text": "Gaps in role-based education, coaching, and hands-on learning opportunities for building confidence to use AI effectively", "response_type": "score", "grounding_span": "**Training and capability-building** – Gaps in role-based education, coaching, and hands-on learning opportunities for building confidence to use AI effectively", "location": "step-2-assess-readiness-across-key-dimensions"}

  "proposed_relationships": [
    {"grounding_span": "AI is only as effective as the data it relies on, making strong data governance and privacy practices foundational to readiness.", "suggested_edge": "is_foundation_for", "from_id": "c_data_governance", "to_id": "c_org_ai_readiness", "location": "1-data-governance-and-privacy"},
    {"grounding_span": "executive behavior — one of the strongest predictors of whether AI initiatives stall or scale", "suggested_edge": "predicts", "from_id": "c_exec_behavior", "to_id": "c_ai_adoption", "location": "why-is-organizational-ai-readiness-important"},
    {"grounding_span": "Overly restrictive policies and complex approval processes can shut down experimentation and slow learning", "suggested_edge": "inhibits", "from_id": "c_governance_control", "to_id": "c_experimentation", "location": "organizational-ai-readiness-challenges"},
    {"grounding_span": "Change management makes AI readiness real by turning AI strategy and governance into employee adoption, proficiency and sustained use", "suggested_edge": "enables", "from_id": "c_change_management", "to_id": "c_org_ai_readiness", "location": "the-role-of-change-management-in-organizational-ai-readiness"},
    {"grounding_span": "When leaders frame readiness as a purely technical exercise — focused on tools, platforms, and infrastructure — they underinvest in change management, communication, leadership behavior, and culture.", "suggested_edge": "undermines", "from_id": "c_org_ai_readiness", "to_id": "c_human_factors", "location": "organizational-ai-readiness-challenges"}
  ]
}
